payload.message: I’m patching the dotfiles stage to do two things: stop importing the direct-home overlay into an already-activated yadm worktree, and run `yadm bootstrap` when the repo provides one. That makes the stage idempotent instead of order-sensitive.

content.text: I’m patching the dotfiles stage to do two things: stop importing the direct-home overlay into an already-activated yadm worktree, and run `yadm bootstrap` when the repo provides one. That makes the stage idempotent instead of order-sensitive.

payload.name: apply_patch
payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: strap/src/lib/dotfiles.sh
@@
 yadm_repo_dir() {
   printf '%s\n' "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}"
 }
 
+dotfiles_bootstrap_path() {
+  printf '%s\n' "$HOME/.config/yadm/bootstrap"
+}
+
+run_yadm_bootstrap_if_present() {
+  local bootstrap_path
+  bootstrap_path="$(dotfiles_bootstrap_path)"
+
+  if [[ -r "$bootstrap_path" ]]; then
+    info "running yadm bootstrap: $bootstrap_path"
+    run yadm bootstrap
+  fi
+}
+
 import_home_tree() {
   local project_root=${1:?project_root}
   local source_home="$project_root/home"
@@
 activate_dotfiles() {
   local project_root=${1:?project_root}
   local repo_url=${DOTFILES_REPO_URL:-}
   local repo_dir
   repo_dir="$(yadm_repo_dir)"
@@
   elif ! command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
     warn 'yadm not found; dry-run continues'
   fi
 
-  if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
-    import_home_tree "$project_root"
-  fi
-
   if [[ -d "$repo_dir" ]]; then
     info 'yadm repo exists; pulling dotfiles'
     run yadm pull --ff-only
+    run_yadm_bootstrap_if_present
     return 0
   fi
 
+  if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
+    import_home_tree "$project_root"
+  fi
+
   if [[ -z "$repo_url" ]]; then
-    warn 'no yadm repo found and DOTFILES_REPO_URL is unset; skipping clone'
-    warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> or run yadm init/clone manually'
+    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
+      warn 'no yadm repo found; direct-home overlay imported without activating yadm'
+      warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> to clone a yadm repo on top of the imported overlay'
+    else
+      warn 'no yadm repo found and DOTFILES_REPO_URL is unset; skipping clone'
+      warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> or run yadm init/clone manually'
+    fi
     return 0
   fi
 
   info "cloning dotfiles into direct HOME tracking: $repo_url"
   run yadm clone "$repo_url"
+  run_yadm_bootstrap_if_present
 }
*** End Patch

payload.message: The test exposed one more detail: `yadm` resolves its bootstrap script under the config directory, not under `~/.config` unconditionally. I’m correcting the lookup so we only invoke bootstrap when `yadm` would actually find an executable script there.

+

-  run_yadm_bootstrap_if_present

payload.message:
Yes. `strap` already reads `DOTFILES_REPO_URL`; the missing piece is exporting it before the dotfiles stage:

```bash
export DOTFILES_REPO_URL="https://github.com/fatb4f/_404"
strap/bootstrap --stages dotfiles,post,doctor
```

I also hardened the dotfiles flow so it no longer overlays `home/` onto an already-activated yadm tree, and it will run an executable repo bootstrap script when one is present.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

yadm_repo_dir() {
  printf '%s\n' "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}"
}

dotfiles_bootstrap_paths() {
  printf '%s\n' \
    "${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/yadm/bootstrap" \
    "$HOME/.config/yadm/bootstrap"
}

run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present() {
  local bootstrap_path

  while IFS= read -r bootstrap_path; do
    [[ -n "$bootstrap_path" ]] || continue
    if [[ -x "$bootstrap_path" ]]; then
      info "running dotfiles bootstrap: $bootstrap_path"
      run "$bootstrap_path"
      return 0
    fi
  done < <(dotfiles_bootstrap_paths)
}

import_home_tree() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local source_home="$project_root/home"

  [[ -d "$source_home" ]] || die "home overlay not found: $source_home"
  require_cmd rsync || die 'rsync is required to import the home overlay'

  info "importing direct-home overlay from $source_home into $HOME"
  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    printf '[dry-run] rsync -a --exclude .git %q/ %q/\n' "$source_home" "$HOME"
  else
    rsync -a --exclude .git "$source_home/" "$HOME/"
  fi
}

activate_dotfiles() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local repo_url=${DOTFILES_REPO_URL:-}
  local repo_dir
  repo_dir="$(yadm_repo_dir)"

  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
    require_cmd yadm || die 'yadm is required for direct $HOME dotfile tracking'
  elif ! command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    warn 'yadm not found; dry-run continues'
  fi

  if [[ -d "$repo_dir" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
      warn 'direct-home overlay import skipped because a yadm repo already exists'
    fi
    info 'yadm repo exists; pulling dotfiles'
    run yadm pull --ff-only
    run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    import_home_tree "$project_root"
  fi

  if [[ -z "$repo_url" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
      warn 'no yadm repo found; direct-home overlay imported without activating yadm'
      warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> to clone a yadm repo on top of the imported overlay'
    else
      warn 'no yadm repo found and DOTFILES_REPO_URL is unset; skipping clone'
      warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> or run yadm init/clone manually'
    fi
    return 0
  fi

  info "cloning dotfiles into direct HOME tracking: $repo_url"
  run yadm clone "$repo_url"
  run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present
}

  info "importing direct-home overlay from $source_home into $HOME"
  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    printf '[dry-run] rsync -a --exclude .git %q/ %q/\n' "$source_home" "$HOME"
  else
    rsync -a --exclude .git "$source_home/" "$HOME/"
  fi
}

activate_dotfiles() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local repo_url=${DOTFILES_REPO_URL:-}
  local repo_dir
  repo_dir="$(yadm_repo_dir)"

  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
    require_cmd yadm || die 'yadm is required for direct $HOME dotfile tracking'
  elif ! command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    warn 'yadm not found; dry-run continues'
  fi

  if [[ -d "$repo_dir" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
      warn 'direct-home overlay import skipped because a yadm repo already exists'
    fi
    info 'yadm repo exists; pulling dotfiles'
    run yadm pull --ff-only
    run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    import_home_tree "$project_root"
  fi

  if [[ -z "$repo_url" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
      warn 'no yadm repo found; direct-home overlay imported without activating yadm'
      warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> to clone a yadm repo on top of the imported overlay'
    else
      warn 'no yadm repo found and DOTFILES_REPO_URL is unset; skipping clone'
      warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> or run yadm init/clone manually'
    fi
    return 0
  fi

  info "cloning dotfiles into direct HOME tracking: $repo_url"
  run yadm clone "$repo_url"
  run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present
}

function zvm_after_init() {
  local up="${terminfo[kcuu1]:-$'\e[A'}"

fi

fi

fi

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

I cleaned up the config path handling and synced the tracked overlay into the live home config.

What changed:
- Normalized `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` in the bootstrap/runtime code.
- Updated the checked-in dotfiles entrypoints to use that normalized config root.
- Copied the tracked `home/.config` overlay plus the root dotfiles into `/home/x404`, so the live `~/.config` now reflects the repo-managed layout.

The only live-only config files still present are `cros-garcon.conf`, `weston.ini`, and the `gh` client state files. I left those alone because they look host/runtime-specific rather than repo-managed.

content.text: How does yadm manage dotfiles source?

payload.message: How does yadm manage dotfiles source?

`yadm` treats your dotfiles repo as a Git repository whose work tree is your home directory.

Practically:
- the Git metadata lives in `~/.local/share/yadm/repo.git`
- the tracked files appear directly under `$HOME`
- `yadm add`, `yadm status`, `yadm commit`, etc. operate on that repo
- `yadm clone <url>` populates the work tree into your home directory
- `yadm bootstrap` runs `$HOME/.config/yadm/bootstrap` if present
- `yadm alt` handles alternate files for host-specific variants

So the “source” of truth is the Git repo, but the checked-out form is your live home tree. In your setup, that means the repo contents should mirror the layout you want under `$HOME`, while `home/` in the `_404` checkout is just this project’s template/source overlay, not yadm’s own storage location.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

REPO_URL="${_404_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/fatb4f/_404.git}"
REPO_ROOT="${_404_REPO_ROOT:-${HOME}/.local/opt/_404}"

die() {
  printf 'error: %s\n' "$*" >&2
  exit 1
}

have() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

install_git() {
  if have git; then
    return 0
  fi

  if have apt-get; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y git
    return 0
  fi

  if have pacman; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm git
    return 0
  fi

  die 'no package manager available to install git'
}

checkout_repo() {
  local repo_parent
  repo_parent="$(dirname -- "$REPO_ROOT")"
  mkdir -p -- "$repo_parent"

  if [[ -d "$REPO_ROOT/.git" ]]; then
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" fetch --all --prune
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" pull --ff-only
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ -e "$REPO_ROOT" ]] && [[ -n "$(find "$REPO_ROOT" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)" ]]; then
    die "refusing to reuse non-git directory: $REPO_ROOT"
  fi

  rm -rf -- "$REPO_ROOT"
  git clone "$REPO_URL" "$REPO_ROOT"
}

main() {
  install_git
  checkout_repo
  exec bash "$REPO_ROOT/strap/bootstrap" "$@"
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

REPO_URL="${_404_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/fatb4f/_404.git}"
REPO_ROOT="${_404_REPO_ROOT:-${HOME}/.local/opt/_404}"

die() {
  printf 'error: %s\n' "$*" >&2
  exit 1
}

have() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

install_git() {
  if have git; then
    return 0
  fi

  if have apt-get; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y git
    return 0
  fi

  if have pacman; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm git
    return 0
  fi

  die 'no package manager available to install git'
}

checkout_repo() {
  local repo_parent
  repo_parent="$(dirname -- "$REPO_ROOT")"
  mkdir -p -- "$repo_parent"

  if [[ -d "$REPO_ROOT/.git" ]]; then
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" fetch --all --prune
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" pull --ff-only
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ -e "$REPO_ROOT" ]] && [[ -n "$(find "$REPO_ROOT" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)" ]]; then
    die "refusing to reuse non-git directory: $REPO_ROOT"
  fi

  rm -rf -- "$REPO_ROOT"
  git clone "$REPO_URL" "$REPO_ROOT"
}

main() {
  install_git
  checkout_repo
  exec bash "$REPO_ROOT/strap/bootstrap" "$@"
}

main "$@"

Output:
# `_404` host bootstrap + direct-HOME dotfiles

This repository is now split by authority boundary:

```text
strap/   host bootstrap and activation adapter
home/    direct $HOME overlay for dotfile import/tracking
```

Bootstrap a new host with:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fatb4f/_404/main/init.sh | bash
```

## Contract

```text
host-bootstrap
  owns: bash-only bootstrap runner, environment seed, system packages, userland tool install
  scope: arch-base | debian-base

userland installer
  owns: GitHub release artifacts under $HOME/.local/opt and $HOME/.local/bin
  exposes: stable symlinks under $HOME/.local/bin

dotfiles
  owns: tracked $HOME state
  manager: yadm first, bare Git possible
  source transform: none
```

## Current flow

```text
acquire repo
  -> strap/bootstrap
      -> detect host class
      -> seed shell env
      -> install system packages/deps
      -> install userland release tools
      -> activate direct-HOME dotfiles
      -> run post-dotfiles package setup
      -> optional chsh after dotfiles are live
      -> doctor
```

## Quick start

Dry-run everything:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run
```

Run only the non-invasive checks:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run --stages detect,env,doctor
```

Install host packages:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages pkgs
```

Install userland release tools:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland
```

Activate dotfiles with yadm:

```bash
DOTFILES_REPO_URL='git@github.com:USER/dotfiles.git' \
  strap/bootstrap --stages dotfiles,post,doctor
```

Seed this repository's `home/` overlay into `$HOME` before yadm activation:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --import-home-tree --stages dotfiles,post,doctor
```

## Direct-HOME migration status

The `home/` tree has been converted away from chezmoi source filenames:

```text
dot_*        -> .* 
executable_* -> real executable name + mode 0755
readonly_*   -> real filename
empty_*      -> real filename
```

That makes `home/` a real `$HOME` overlay rather than a chezmoi source tree.

## Bootstrap shell

Bootstrap scripts run non-interactively under `bash`.

`zsh` is a system dependency, not a bootstrap prerequisite. It is installed by the package-manager stage and becomes the login shell only after the dotfiles layer is live.

## Host classes

Only two base classes are active:

| Class | Package adapter | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `arch-base` | `pacman` | Arch, Artix, EndeavourOS-style systems |
| `debian-base` | `apt-get` | Debian, Ubuntu, Penguin/Borealis-style systems |

ChromeOS/Penguin-specific integration should be added later as an overlay on `debian-base`.

## Important files

```text
strap/bootstrap                         entrypoint
strap/src/init.sh                       coordinator
strap/src/lib/which_host.sh             host-class detection
strap/src/lib/pkgs/arch-base.pkgs       Arch package overlay
strap/src/lib/pkgs/debian-base.pkgs     Debian package overlay
strap/src/lib/userland/tools.tsv        GitHub release userland tools
strap/contracts/*.cue                   authority contracts
home/                                   direct $HOME overlay
```

## Next cut

The next high-signal work is to harden the GitHub release installer:

1. add checksums/attestation fields to `strap/src/lib/pkgs/artifacts.json`
2. split `penguin` into a `debian-base + chromeos-vm` overlay
3. add a `bare-git` dotfile backend beside the current `yadm` backend

The `arch-usb` workflow now assembles a Submarine-shaped external disk image
from a cached `arch-rootfs.ext4` artifact and the `submarine-x86.kpart`
payload. The rootfs stage is built separately before image assembly, and the
final image uses an ext4 root partition.

payload.aggregated_output:
# `_404` host bootstrap + direct-HOME dotfiles

This repository is now split by authority boundary:

```text
strap/   host bootstrap and activation adapter
home/    direct $HOME overlay for dotfile import/tracking
```

Bootstrap a new host with:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fatb4f/_404/main/init.sh | bash
```

## Contract

```text
host-bootstrap
  owns: bash-only bootstrap runner, environment seed, system packages, userland tool install
  scope: arch-base | debian-base

userland installer
  owns: GitHub release artifacts under $HOME/.local/opt and $HOME/.local/bin
  exposes: stable symlinks under $HOME/.local/bin

dotfiles
  owns: tracked $HOME state
  manager: yadm first, bare Git possible
  source transform: none
```

## Current flow

```text
acquire repo
  -> strap/bootstrap
      -> detect host class
      -> seed shell env
      -> install system packages/deps
      -> install userland release tools
      -> activate direct-HOME dotfiles
      -> run post-dotfiles package setup
      -> optional chsh after dotfiles are live
      -> doctor
```

## Quick start

Dry-run everything:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run
```

Run only the non-invasive checks:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run --stages detect,env,doctor
```

Install host packages:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages pkgs
```

Install userland release tools:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland
```

Activate dotfiles with yadm:

```bash
DOTFILES_REPO_URL='git@github.com:USER/dotfiles.git' \
  strap/bootstrap --stages dotfiles,post,doctor
```

Seed this repository's `home/` overlay into `$HOME` before yadm activation:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --import-home-tree --stages dotfiles,post,doctor
```

## Direct-HOME migration status

The `home/` tree has been converted away from chezmoi source filenames:

```text
dot_*        -> .* 
executable_* -> real executable name + mode 0755
readonly_*   -> real filename
empty_*      -> real filename
```

That makes `home/` a real `$HOME` overlay rather than a chezmoi source tree.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

REPO_URL="${_404_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/fatb4f/_404.git}"
REPO_ROOT="${_404_REPO_ROOT:-${HOME}/.local/share/opt/_404}"

die() {
  printf 'error: %s\n' "$*" >&2
  exit 1
}

have() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

install_git() {
  if have git; then
    return 0
  fi

  if have apt-get; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y git
    return 0
  fi

  if have pacman; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm git
    return 0
  fi

  die 'no package manager available to install git'
}

checkout_repo() {
  local repo_parent
  repo_parent="$(dirname -- "$REPO_ROOT")"
  mkdir -p -- "$repo_parent"

  if [[ -d "$REPO_ROOT/.git" ]]; then
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" fetch --all --prune
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" pull --ff-only
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ -e "$REPO_ROOT" ]] && [[ -n "$(find "$REPO_ROOT" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)" ]]; then
    die "refusing to reuse non-git directory: $REPO_ROOT"
  fi

  rm -rf -- "$REPO_ROOT"
  git clone "$REPO_URL" "$REPO_ROOT"
}

main() {
  install_git
  checkout_repo
  exec bash "$REPO_ROOT/strap/bootstrap" "$@"
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

yadm_repo_dir() {
  printf '%s\n' "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}"
}

dotfiles_bootstrap_paths() {
  local config_home
  config_home="$(xdg_config_home)"

  printf '%s\n' "$config_home/yadm/bootstrap"
}

run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present() {
  local bootstrap_path

  while IFS= read -r bootstrap_path; do
    [[ -n "$bootstrap_path" ]] || continue
    if [[ -x "$bootstrap_path" ]]; then
      info "running dotfiles bootstrap: $bootstrap_path"
      run "$bootstrap_path"
      return 0
    fi
  done < <(dotfiles_bootstrap_paths)
}

import_home_tree() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local source_home="$project_root/home"

  [[ -d "$source_home" ]] || die "home overlay not found: $source_home"
  require_cmd rsync || die 'rsync is required to import the home overlay'

  info "importing direct-home overlay from $source_home into $HOME"
  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    printf '[dry-run] rsync -a --exclude .git %q/ %q/\n' "$source_home" "$HOME"
  else
    rsync -a --exclude .git "$source_home/" "$HOME/"
  fi
}

activate_dotfiles() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local repo_url=${DOTFILES_REPO_URL:-}
  local repo_dir
  repo_dir="$(yadm_repo_dir)"

  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
    require_cmd yadm || die 'yadm is required for direct $HOME dotfile tracking'
  elif ! command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    warn 'yadm not found; dry-run continues'
  fi

set -euo pipefail

REPO_URL="${_404_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/fatb4f/_404.git}"

die() {

have() {

}

install_git() {

    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y git
    return 0

  if have pacman; then

    sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm git

  die 'no package manager available to install git'
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

yadm_repo_dir() {
  printf '%s\n' "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}"
}

dotfiles_bootstrap_paths() {
  local config_home
  config_home="$(xdg_config_home)"

  printf '%s\n' "$config_home/yadm/bootstrap"
}

run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present() {
  local bootstrap_path

  while IFS= read -r bootstrap_path; do
    [[ -n "$bootstrap_path" ]] || continue
    if [[ -x "$bootstrap_path" ]]; then
      info "running dotfiles bootstrap: $bootstrap_path"
      run "$bootstrap_path"
      return 0
    fi
  done < <(dotfiles_bootstrap_paths)
}

import_home_tree() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local source_home="$project_root/home"

  [[ -d "$source_home" ]] || die "home overlay not found: $source_home"
  require_cmd rsync || die 'rsync is required to import the home overlay'

  info "importing direct-home overlay from $source_home into $HOME"
  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    printf '[dry-run] rsync -a --exclude .git %q/ %q/\n' "$source_home" "$HOME"
  else
    rsync -a --exclude .git "$source_home/" "$HOME/"
  fi
}

activate_dotfiles() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local repo_url=${DOTFILES_REPO_URL:-}
  local repo_dir
  repo_dir="$(yadm_repo_dir)"

  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
    require_cmd yadm || die 'yadm is required for direct $HOME dotfile tracking'
  elif ! command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    warn 'yadm not found; dry-run continues'
  fi

  if [[ -d "$repo_dir" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
      warn 'direct-home overlay import skipped because a yadm repo already exists'
    fi
    info 'yadm repo exists; pulling dotfiles'
    run yadm pull --ff-only
    run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    import_home_tree "$project_root"
  fi

  if [[ -z "$repo_url" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
      warn 'no yadm repo found; direct-home overlay imported without activating yadm'
      warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> to clone a yadm repo on top of the imported overlay'
    else

      warn 'set DOTFILES_REPO_URL=<repo-url> or run yadm init/clone manually'
    fi
    return 0
  fi

  info "cloning dotfiles into direct HOME tracking: $repo_url"
  run yadm clone "$repo_url"
  run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present
}

payload.message:
No. `_404/home` is the source overlay, not a yadm repository.

The roles are different:

- `_404/home` is a plain directory in the bootstrap checkout.
- yadm’s repo lives in `~/.local/share/yadm/repo.git` by default.
- yadm tracks the live `$HOME` tree, not the `_404/home` directory.

So pointing `DOTFILES_REPO_URL` at `_404/home` would not work, because `home/` is not a Git repo.

If the goal is to make bootstrap self-contained from the local `_404` checkout, the right shape is:
1. use `REPO_ROOT/home` as the import source
2. either `yadm init` in the user’s yadm repo location, or skip yadm unless an actual dotfiles repo exists
3. do not reuse `DOTFILES_REPO_URL` for the `_404` checkout path

Right now that variable should be removed or repurposed, because the local checkout is already available through `REPO_ROOT`.

content.text: or move the bootstrap into the dotfiles portion of the repo?

payload.message: or move the bootstrap into the dotfiles portion of the repo?

Not the whole bootstrap. Keep these separate:

- `init.sh` at repo root: fetches the `_404` checkout and starts the bootstrap.
- `strap/bootstrap`: host orchestration.
- `home/.config/yadm/bootstrap`: yadm’s own post-clone hook, if you want repo-local dotfile setup.

If your goal is to remove the remote URL entirely, moving `strap/` into the dotfiles tree is the wrong fix. `yadm` manages the live `$HOME` worktree; it does not make `_404/home` into a Git repo by itself. The clean path is either:

1. keep the current split and default the dotfiles stage to the local `_404` checkout as its source, or
2. add a real yadm bootstrap flow that seeds/initializes the user’s yadm repo from `home/`.

So yes, move dotfiles-specific bootstrap logic into `home/.config/yadm/bootstrap` if needed. No, don’t move the host bootstrap there. That would blur the boundary and make the startup order worse.

  esac
done

# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/common.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/common.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/arch_host.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/arch_host.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/debian_host.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/debian_host.sh"

# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/dotfiles.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/dotfiles.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/post.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/post.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/doctor.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/doctor.sh"

usage() {
  cat <<'USAGE'
usage: strap/bootstrap [options]

Options:
  --dry-run                 Print actions without applying changes.
  --host CLASS              Override detected host class: arch-base|debian-base.
  --stages CSV              Run selected stages. Default: env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,doctor.
                            Available: detect,env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,chsh,doctor.
  --dotfiles-repo URL       yadm remote to clone when no yadm repo exists.
  --import-home-tree        Import ./home as a direct $HOME overlay before yadm activation.
  --force                   Reinstall userland tools even if links already exist.
  -h, --help                Show this help.

Environment:
  DOTFILES_REPO_URL         Same as --dotfiles-repo.
  _404_LOCAL_BIN            Override userland install directory. Default: ~/.local/bin.
  _404_ARTIFACT_CACHE       Override artifact download cache.
USAGE
}

DRY_RUN=${DRY_RUN:-0}
FORCE=${FORCE:-0}
IMPORT_HOME_TREE=${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}
HOST_CLASS=${HOST_CLASS:-}
STAGES=${STAGES:-env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,doctor}

while (($#)); do
  case "$1" in
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=1
      shift
      ;;
    --force)
      FORCE=1
      shift
      ;;
    --host)
      HOST_CLASS=${2:-}
      [[ -n "$HOST_CLASS" ]] || die '--host requires a value'
      shift 2
      ;;
    --stages|--stage)
      STAGES=${2:-}
      [[ -n "$STAGES" ]] || die '--stages requires a comma-separated value'
      shift 2
      ;;
    --dotfiles-repo)
      DOTFILES_REPO_URL=${2:-}
      [[ -n "$DOTFILES_REPO_URL" ]] || die '--dotfiles-repo requires a URL'
      shift 2
      ;;
    --import-home-tree)
      IMPORT_HOME_TREE=1
      shift
      ;;
    -h|--help)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      die "unknown argument: $1"
      ;;
  esac
done

export DRY_RUN FORCE IMPORT_HOME_TREE DOTFILES_REPO_URL STRAP_ROOT

if [[ -z "$HOST_CLASS" ]]; then
  HOST_CLASS="$("$LIB_DIR/which_host.sh")" || die 'host detection failed'
fi

case "$HOST_CLASS" in
  arch-base|debian-base) ;;
  *) die "unsupported host class: $HOST_CLASS" ;;
esac

stage_enabled() {
  local needle=${1:?stage}
  case ",$STAGES," in
    *",$needle,"*) return 0 ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac
}

validate_stages() {
  local IFS=',' stage
  for stage in $STAGES; do
    case "$stage" in
      detect|env|pkgs|userland|dotfiles|post|chsh|doctor) ;;

## Direct-HOME migration status

The `home/` tree has been converted away from chezmoi source filenames:

```text
dot_*        -> .* 
executable_* -> real executable name + mode 0755
readonly_*   -> real filename
empty_*      -> real filename
```

That makes `home/` a real `$HOME` overlay rather than a chezmoi source tree.

## Package-manager deps

`jq`, `gh`, and `npm` are package-manager dependencies, not userland release artifacts.
There is no shared `common.pkgs` layer; package names are adapter-specific.
`base-devel` is the shared build-tool intent; on Arch it resolves to `base-devel`, and on Debian it resolves through `build-essential`.
`ya pkg install` runs in the post-dotfiles stage so tracked Yazi package manifests can hydrate after dotfiles activation.

payload.message:
Yes. I’ve aligned the bootstrap with that model:

- the `_404` checkout is the single source repo
- `init.sh` clones it into `~/.local/share/opt/_404`
- `strap/bootstrap` uses that local checkout’s `home/` tree as the dotfiles source
- the old `DOTFILES_REPO_URL` / `--dotfiles-repo` path is gone

So the dotfiles flow no longer needs a remote URL. It imports from the local checkout and, if a yadm repo already exists, it pulls that repo; otherwise it just seeds the home overlay from `_404/home`.

I also updated the help text and docs to stop referring to the removed URL flow.

content.text: And yadm won't have any issues tracking the dotfiles?

payload.message: And yadm won't have any issues tracking the dotfiles?

`yadm` itself will be fine tracking the dotfiles as long as the files end up in `$HOME` with the layout you want. It does not care that the source came from `_404/home`; it only cares about the live worktree in your home directory.

The important caveat is this: the current bootstrap no longer creates a yadm repo for you when none exists. So:

- if `~/.local/share/yadm/repo.git` already exists, `yadm pull` will keep tracking the live files
- if it does not exist, bootstrap just copies `_404/home` into `$HOME` and stops there

So there is no inherent yadm problem with the dotfiles layout, but there is a bootstrap problem if you expect yadm to start tracking automatically on a fresh machine. In that case, the bootstrap still needs an explicit `yadm init` or `yadm clone` step before or after the import.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

REPO_URL="${_404_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/fatb4f/_404.git}"
REPO_ROOT="${_404_REPO_ROOT:-${HOME}/.local/share/opt/_404}"

die() {
  printf 'error: %s\n' "$*" >&2
  exit 1
}

have() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

install_git() {
  if have git; then
    return 0
  fi

  if have apt-get; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y git
    return 0
  fi

  if have pacman; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm git
    return 0
  fi

  die 'no package manager available to install git'
}

checkout_repo() {
  local repo_parent
  repo_parent="$(dirname -- "$REPO_ROOT")"
  mkdir -p -- "$repo_parent"

  if [[ -d "$REPO_ROOT/.git" ]]; then
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" fetch --all --prune
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" pull --ff-only
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ -e "$REPO_ROOT" ]] && [[ -n "$(find "$REPO_ROOT" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)" ]]; then
    die "refusing to reuse non-git directory: $REPO_ROOT"
  fi

  rm -rf -- "$REPO_ROOT"
  git clone "$REPO_URL" "$REPO_ROOT"
}

main() {
  install_git
  checkout_repo
  exec bash "$REPO_ROOT/strap/bootstrap" "$@"
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

STRAP_ROOT="$(CDPATH= cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd -P)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(CDPATH= cd -- "$STRAP_ROOT/../.." && pwd -P)"
LIB_DIR="$STRAP_ROOT/lib"
PKG_DIR="$LIB_DIR/pkgs"
USERLAND_MANIFEST="$PKG_DIR/artifacts.json"

# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/common.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/common.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/arch_host.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/arch_host.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/debian_host.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/debian_host.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/bootstrap_env.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/bootstrap_env.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/zsh.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/zsh.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/system_pkgs.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/system_pkgs.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/userland.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/userland.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/dotfiles.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/dotfiles.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/post.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/post.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/doctor.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/doctor.sh"

usage() {
  cat <<'USAGE'
usage: strap/bootstrap [options]

Options:
  --dry-run                 Print actions without applying changes.
  --host CLASS              Override detected host class: arch-base|debian-base.
  --stages CSV              Run selected stages. Default: env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,doctor.
                            Available: detect,env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,chsh,doctor.
  --import-home-tree        Import ./home as a direct $HOME overlay before dotfile activation.
  --force                   Reinstall userland tools even if links already exist.
  -h, --help                Show this help.

Environment:
  _404_LOCAL_BIN            Override userland install directory. Default: ~/.local/bin.
  _404_ARTIFACT_CACHE       Override artifact download cache.
USAGE
}

DRY_RUN=${DRY_RUN:-0}
FORCE=${FORCE:-0}
IMPORT_HOME_TREE=${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}
HOST_CLASS=${HOST_CLASS:-}
STAGES=${STAGES:-env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,doctor}

while (($#)); do
  case "$1" in
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=1
      shift
      ;;
    --force)
      FORCE=1
      shift
      ;;
    --host)
      HOST_CLASS=${2:-}
      [[ -n "$HOST_CLASS" ]] || die '--host requires a value'
      shift 2
      ;;
    --stages|--stage)
      STAGES=${2:-}
      [[ -n "$STAGES" ]] || die '--stages requires a comma-separated value'
      shift 2
      ;;
    --import-home-tree)
      IMPORT_HOME_TREE=1
      shift
      ;;
    -h|--help)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      die "unknown argument: $1"
      ;;
  esac
done

export DRY_RUN FORCE IMPORT_HOME_TREE STRAP_ROOT

if [[ -z "$HOST_CLASS" ]]; then
  HOST_CLASS="$("$LIB_DIR/which_host.sh")" || die 'host detection failed'
fi

case "$HOST_CLASS" in
  arch-base|debian-base) ;;
  *) die "unsupported host class: $HOST_CLASS" ;;
esac

stage_enabled() {
  local needle=${1:?stage}
  case ",$STAGES," in
    *",$needle,"*) return 0 ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac
}

validate_stages() {
  local IFS=',' stage
  for stage in $STAGES; do
    case "$stage" in
      detect|env|pkgs|userland|dotfiles|post|chsh|doctor) ;;
      *) die "unknown stage: $stage" ;;
    esac
  done
}

validate_stages

info "project_root=$PROJECT_ROOT"
info "host_class=$HOST_CLASS"
info "stages=$STAGES"

if stage_enabled detect; then
  printf '%s\n' "$HOST_CLASS"
fi

if stage_enabled env; then
  bootstrap_env "$LIB_DIR/shell" "$HOME"
fi


if stage_enabled pkgs; then
  install_system_packages "$HOST_CLASS" "$PKG_DIR"
fi

if stage_enabled userland; then
  install_userland_tools "$USERLAND_MANIFEST"
fi

if stage_enabled dotfiles; then
  activate_dotfiles "$PROJECT_ROOT"
fi

if stage_enabled post; then
  post_dotfiles "$PROJECT_ROOT"
fi

if stage_enabled chsh; then
  set_login_shell_after_dotfiles
fi

if stage_enabled doctor; then
  doctor "$PROJECT_ROOT" "$HOST_CLASS"
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

yadm_repo_dir() {
  printf '%s\n' "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}"
}

dotfiles_bootstrap_paths() {
  local config_home
  config_home="$(xdg_config_home)"

  printf '%s\n' "$config_home/yadm/bootstrap"
}

run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present() {
  local bootstrap_path

  while IFS= read -r bootstrap_path; do
    [[ -n "$bootstrap_path" ]] || continue
    if [[ -x "$bootstrap_path" ]]; then
      info "running dotfiles bootstrap: $bootstrap_path"
      run "$bootstrap_path"
      return 0
    fi
  done < <(dotfiles_bootstrap_paths)
}

import_home_tree() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local source_home="$project_root/home"

  [[ -d "$source_home" ]] || die "home overlay not found: $source_home"
  require_cmd rsync || die 'rsync is required to import the home overlay'

  info "importing direct-home overlay from $source_home into $HOME"
  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    printf '[dry-run] rsync -a --exclude .git %q/ %q/\n' "$source_home" "$HOME"
  else
    rsync -a --exclude .git "$source_home/" "$HOME/"
  fi
}

activate_dotfiles() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local repo_dir
  repo_dir="$(yadm_repo_dir)"

  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
    require_cmd yadm || die 'yadm is required for direct $HOME dotfile tracking'
  elif ! command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    warn 'yadm not found; dry-run continues'
  fi

  if [[ -d "$repo_dir" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
      warn 'direct-home overlay import skipped because a yadm repo already exists'
    fi
    info 'yadm repo exists; pulling dotfiles'
    run yadm pull --ff-only
    run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present
    return 0
  fi

  import_home_tree "$project_root"
  warn 'no yadm repo found; imported direct-home overlay from the local _404 checkout'
}

  rm -rf -- "$REPO_ROOT"
  git clone "$REPO_URL" "$REPO_ROOT"
}

main() {
  install_git
  checkout_repo
  exec bash "$REPO_ROOT/strap/bootstrap" "$@"
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

STRAP_ROOT="$(CDPATH= cd -- "$(dirname -- "$0")" && pwd -P)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(CDPATH= cd -- "$STRAP_ROOT/../.." && pwd -P)"
LIB_DIR="$STRAP_ROOT/lib"
PKG_DIR="$LIB_DIR/pkgs"
USERLAND_MANIFEST="$PKG_DIR/artifacts.json"

# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/common.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/common.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/arch_host.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/arch_host.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/debian_host.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/debian_host.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/bootstrap_env.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/bootstrap_env.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/zsh.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/zsh.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/system_pkgs.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/system_pkgs.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/userland.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/userland.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/dotfiles.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/dotfiles.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/post.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/post.sh"
# shellcheck source=strap/src/lib/doctor.sh
. "$LIB_DIR/doctor.sh"

usage() {
  cat <<'USAGE'
usage: strap/bootstrap [options]

Options:
  --dry-run                 Print actions without applying changes.
  --host CLASS              Override detected host class: arch-base|debian-base.
  --stages CSV              Run selected stages. Default: env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,doctor.
                            Available: detect,env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,chsh,doctor.
  --import-home-tree        Import ./home as a direct $HOME overlay before dotfile activation.
  --force                   Reinstall userland tools even if links already exist.
  -h, --help                Show this help.

Environment:
  _404_LOCAL_BIN            Override userland install directory. Default: ~/.local/bin.
  _404_ARTIFACT_CACHE       Override artifact download cache.
USAGE
}

DRY_RUN=${DRY_RUN:-0}
FORCE=${FORCE:-0}
IMPORT_HOME_TREE=${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}
HOST_CLASS=${HOST_CLASS:-}
STAGES=${STAGES:-env,pkgs,userland,dotfiles,post,doctor}

while (($#)); do
  case "$1" in
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=1
      shift
      ;;
    --force)
      FORCE=1
      shift
      ;;
    --host)
      HOST_CLASS=${2:-}
      [[ -n "$HOST_CLASS" ]] || die '--host requires a value'
      shift 2
      ;;
    --stages|--stage)
      STAGES=${2:-}
      [[ -n "$STAGES" ]] || die '--stages requires a comma-separated value'
      shift 2
      ;;
    --import-home-tree)
      IMPORT_HOME_TREE=1
      shift
      ;;
    -h|--help)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      die "unknown argument: $1"
      ;;
  esac
done

export DRY_RUN FORCE IMPORT_HOME_TREE STRAP_ROOT

if [[ -z "$HOST_CLASS" ]]; then
  HOST_CLASS="$("$LIB_DIR/which_host.sh")" || die 'host detection failed'
fi

case "$HOST_CLASS" in
  arch-base|debian-base) ;;
  *) die "unsupported host class: $HOST_CLASS" ;;
esac

stage_enabled() {
  local needle=${1:?stage}
  case ",$STAGES," in
    *",$needle,"*) return 0 ;;
    *) return 1 ;;
  esac
}

validate_stages() {
  local IFS=',' stage
  for stage in $STAGES; do
    case "$stage" in
      detect|env|pkgs|userland|dotfiles|post|chsh|doctor) ;;
      *) die "unknown stage: $stage" ;;
    esac
  done
}

validate_stages

info "project_root=$PROJECT_ROOT"
info "host_class=$HOST_CLASS"
info "stages=$STAGES"

if stage_enabled detect; then
  printf '%s\n' "$HOST_CLASS"
fi

if stage_enabled env; then
  bootstrap_env "$LIB_DIR/shell" "$HOME"
fi


if stage_enabled pkgs; then
  install_system_packages "$HOST_CLASS" "$PKG_DIR"
fi

if stage_enabled userland; then
  install_userland_tools "$USERLAND_MANIFEST"
fi

if stage_enabled dotfiles; then
  activate_dotfiles "$PROJECT_ROOT"
fi

if stage_enabled post; then
  post_dotfiles "$PROJECT_ROOT"
fi

if stage_enabled chsh; then
  set_login_shell_after_dotfiles
fi

if stage_enabled doctor; then
  doctor "$PROJECT_ROOT" "$HOST_CLASS"
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

yadm_repo_dir() {
  printf '%s\n' "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}"
}

dotfiles_bootstrap_paths() {
  local config_home
  config_home="$(xdg_config_home)"

  printf '%s\n' "$config_home/yadm/bootstrap"
}

run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present() {
  local bootstrap_path

  while IFS= read -r bootstrap_path; do
    [[ -n "$bootstrap_path" ]] || continue
    if [[ -x "$bootstrap_path" ]]; then
      info "running dotfiles bootstrap: $bootstrap_path"
      run "$bootstrap_path"
      return 0
    fi
  done < <(dotfiles_bootstrap_paths)
}

import_home_tree() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local source_home="$project_root/home"

  [[ -d "$source_home" ]] || die "home overlay not found: $source_home"
  require_cmd rsync || die 'rsync is required to import the home overlay'

  info "importing direct-home overlay from $source_home into $HOME"
  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
    printf '[dry-run] rsync -a --exclude .git %q/ %q/\n' "$source_home" "$HOME"
  else
    rsync -a --exclude .git "$source_home/" "$HOME/"
  fi
}

activate_dotfiles() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local repo_dir
  repo_dir="$(yadm_repo_dir)"

  if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
    require_cmd yadm || die 'yadm is required for direct $HOME dotfile tracking'
  elif ! command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    warn 'yadm not found; dry-run continues'
  fi

  if [[ -d "$repo_dir" ]]; then
    if [[ "${IMPORT_HOME_TREE:-0}" == 1 ]]; then
      warn 'direct-home overlay import skipped because a yadm repo already exists'
    fi
    info 'yadm repo exists; pulling dotfiles'
    run yadm pull --ff-only
    run_dotfiles_bootstrap_if_present
    return 0
  fi

  import_home_tree "$project_root"
  warn 'no yadm repo found; imported direct-home overlay from the local _404 checkout'
}

# `_404` host bootstrap + direct-HOME dotfiles

This repository is now split by authority boundary:

```text
strap/   host bootstrap and activation adapter
home/    direct $HOME overlay for dotfile import/tracking
```

Bootstrap a new host with:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fatb4f/_404/main/init.sh | bash
```

## Contract

```text
host-bootstrap
  owns: bash-only bootstrap runner, environment seed, system packages, userland tool install
  scope: arch-base | debian-base

userland installer
  owns: GitHub release artifacts under $HOME/.local/opt and $HOME/.local/bin
  exposes: stable symlinks under $HOME/.local/bin

dotfiles
  owns: tracked $HOME state
  manager: yadm first, bare Git possible
  source transform: none
```

## Current flow

```text
acquire repo
  -> strap/bootstrap
      -> detect host class
      -> seed shell env
      -> install system packages/deps
      -> install userland release tools
      -> activate direct-HOME dotfiles
      -> run post-dotfiles package setup
      -> optional chsh after dotfiles are live
      -> doctor
```

## Quick start

Dry-run everything:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run
```

Run only the non-invasive checks:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run --stages detect,env,doctor
```

Install host packages:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages pkgs
```

Install userland release tools:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland
```

Activate dotfiles from the local `_404` checkout:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages dotfiles,post,doctor
```

Seed this repository's `home/` overlay into `$HOME` before dotfile activation:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --import-home-tree --stages dotfiles,post,doctor
```

## Direct-HOME migration status

The `home/` tree has been converted away from chezmoi source filenames:

```text
dot_*        -> .* 
executable_* -> real executable name + mode 0755
readonly_*   -> real filename
empty_*      -> real filename
```

That makes `home/` a real `$HOME` overlay rather than a chezmoi source tree.

## Bootstrap shell

Bootstrap scripts run non-interactively under `bash`.

`zsh` is a system dependency, not a bootstrap prerequisite. It is installed by the package-manager stage and becomes the login shell only after the dotfiles layer is live.

## Host classes

Only two base classes are active:

| Class | Package adapter | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `arch-base` | `pacman` | Arch, Artix, EndeavourOS-style systems |
| `debian-base` | `apt-get` | Debian, Ubuntu, Penguin/Borealis-style systems |

ChromeOS/Penguin-specific integration should be added later as an overlay on `debian-base`.

## Important files

```text
strap/bootstrap                         entrypoint
strap/src/init.sh                       coordinator

Original token count: 803
Output:
# `_404` strap

Host bootstrap is separate from dotfile state.

```text
host-bootstrap
  -> bash-only non-interactive runner
  -> minimal environment files
  -> arch-base/debian-base package deps
  -> GitHub-release userland tools
  -> direct-HOME dotfiles
  -> post-dotfiles package setup
  -> optional chsh after dotfiles are live
```

The dotfile authority should track `$HOME` directly with `yadm` or a bare Git repo.

## Host classes

Only two base classes are supported for now:

| Host class | Adapter |
|---|---|
| `arch-base` | `sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm` |
| `debian-base` | `sudo -n apt-get`, `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` |

Penguin/Borealis-style Debian environments collapse to `debian-base`; ChromeOS-specific integration should be added later as an overlay, not as a third base class.

## Non-interactive contract

`strap/bootstrap` runs under Bash and never prompts intentionally.

Package managers use non-interactive flags:

```text
arch-base   -> sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm
debian-base -> sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y
```

`chsh` runs only after dotfiles are live, and it is not part of the default stage list. If it cannot run non-interactively, it prints the manual command and continues with a warning.

Before install, each base-package entry is smoke-tested against the package manager to make sure it resolves in the current distro metadata. If a package does not resolve, bootstrap fails closed before the install step.

## Commands

Dry-run the full flow:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run
```

Run only host detection:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages detect
```

Seed Bash-compatible env files and run doctor:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages env,doctor
```

Install system packages only:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages pkgs
```

Install userland release tools only:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland
```

Activate dotfiles from the local checkout, then attempt non-interactive `chsh`:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages dotfiles,post,chsh,doctor
```

Import the repository `home/` overlay into `$HOME` before dotfile activation:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --import-home-tree --stages dotfiles,post,chsh,doctor
```

## Package-manager deps

`jq`, `gh`, and `npm` are package-manager dependencies, not userland release artifacts.
There is no shared `common.pkgs` layer; package names are adapter-specific.
`base-devel` is the shared build-tool intent; on Arch it resolves to `base-devel`, and on Debian it resolves through `build-essential`.
`ya pkg install` runs in the post-dotfiles stage so tracked Yazi package manifests can hydrate after dotfiles activation.

Concrete package lists:

```text
strap/src/lib/pkgs/arch-base.pkgs
strap/src/lib/pkgs/debian-base.pkgs
```

The package lists stay host-specific because Arch and Debian package names diverge.

## Userland artifacts

Userland packages are declared in:

```text
strap/src/lib/pkgs/artifacts.json
```

The installer filters that manifest with `jq`:

```bash
jq -c '.artifacts[] | select((.enabled // true) == true)' \
  strap/src/lib/pkgs/artifacts.json
```

For each selected row, it downloads the latest matching GitHub release asset with:

payload.name: apply_patch
payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: ../strap/README.md
@@
 Host bootstrap is separate from dotfile state.
 
 ```text
 host-bootstrap
   -> bash-only non-interactive runner
   -> minimal environment files
   -> arch-base/debian-base package deps
   -> GitHub-release userland tools
-  -> direct-HOME dotfiles
-  -> post-dotfiles package setup
-  -> optional chsh after dotfiles are live
+  -> doctor
 ```
 
-The dotfile authority should track `$HOME` directly with `yadm` or a bare Git repo.
+The dotfile authority should track `$HOME` directly with `yadm` or a bare Git repo.
@@
-`chsh` runs only after dotfiles are live, and it is not part of the default stage list. If it cannot run non-interactively, it prints the manual command and continues with a warning.
-
 Before install, each base-package entry is smoke-tested against the package manager to make sure it resolves in the current distro metadata. If a package does not resolve, bootstrap fails closed before the install step.
@@
 Seed Bash-compatible env files and run doctor:
 
 ```bash
 strap/bootstrap --stages env,doctor
 ```
@@
 Install userland release tools only:
 
 ```bash
 strap/bootstrap --stages userland
 ```
-
-Activate dotfiles from the local checkout, then attempt non-interactive `chsh`:
-
-```bash
-strap/bootstrap --stages dotfiles,post,chsh,doctor
-```
-
-Import the repository `home/` overlay into `$HOME` before dotfile activation:
-
-```bash
-strap/bootstrap --import-home-tree --stages dotfiles,post,chsh,doctor
-```
 
 ## Package-manager deps
 
 `jq`, `gh`, and `npm` are package-manager dependencies, not userland release artifacts.
 There is no shared `common.pkgs` layer; package names are adapter-specific.
 `base-devel` is the shared build-tool intent; on Arch it resolves to `base-devel`, and on Debian it resolves through `build-essential`.
-`ya pkg install` runs in the post-dotfiles stage so tracked Yazi package manifests can hydrate after dotfiles activation.
 
 Concrete package lists:
*** End Patch

content.text: So with this revised workflow, when bootstrapping completes, the remaining task is yadm init from _404/home?

payload.message: So with this revised workflow, when bootstrapping completes, the remaining task is yadm init from _404/home?

Yes, with the revised split, bootstrap ends after host setup. The remaining dotfiles work is separate yadm work.

The practical next step is:

1. initialize yadm in the live home if it does not already exist
2. copy or rsync `_404/home` into `$HOME`
3. `yadm add` and commit, or pull if the repo already exists

So it is not literally `yadm init from _404/home`; `yadm` operates on `$HOME` as its worktree. `_404/home` is just the source overlay you use to seed that worktree.

payload.message:
Not in the `chezmoi init <repo>` sense.

yadm’s documented model is:

- `yadm init` creates a new empty repo for `$HOME`
- `yadm clone <url>` clones an existing dotfiles repo into `$HOME` as the worktree

That’s straight from the getting-started docs and overview. yadm’s repo is the dotfiles repo; `$HOME` is the worktree. It does not have a separate “import this source tree into managed dotfiles” command like chezmoi does. Sources: [Getting Started](https://yadm.io/docs/getting_started), [Overview](https://yadm.io/docs/overview).

So for your setup:

- `_404/home` is just the source overlay inside the checkout
- yadm tracks files in the live `$HOME`
- if you want yadm to manage those files, you seed them into `$HOME` and then use yadm on `$HOME`

One nuance: if you already have a Git repo that contains dotfiles, `yadm clone` is still the yadm-native way to bring it into a yadm-managed `$HOME`. But that is cloning a repo, not importing a plain directory tree.

content.text: I have that if we refactor the _404 repo

payload.message: I have that if we refactor the _404 repo

Yes. That’s the real path if you want `yadm`-style management without a remote dotfiles repo.

The refactor would be:
- keep `_404` as the single Git repo you clone locally
- make `home/` the authoritative dotfile source tree
- add a separate yadm bootstrap or init step that turns the live `$HOME` into the yadm worktree, seeded from that local `home/` tree

What yadm does not give you is a direct “import this plain directory tree as a repo” command. So the repo has to be shaped to support one of these:
- `yadm clone` from an actual dotfiles Git repo
- `yadm init` plus a script that copies `home/` into `$HOME` and then tracks it

Given your constraint that bootstrap should stay host-only, the clean split is:
- `init.sh`: clone `_404`
- `strap/bootstrap`: host setup only
- a yadm-specific step, separate from bootstrap, that seeds or manages dotfiles from `_404/home`

That is a repo refactor, not a yadm feature.

Output:
# `_404` host bootstrap + dotfiles source

This repository is now split by authority boundary:

```text
strap/   host bootstrap and activation adapter
home/    direct $HOME overlay for yadm tracking
```

Bootstrap a new host with:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fatb4f/_404/main/init.sh | bash
```

## Contract

```text
host-bootstrap
  owns: bash-only bootstrap runner, environment seed, system packages, userland tool install, doctor
  scope: arch-base | debian-base

userland installer
  owns: GitHub release artifacts under $HOME/.local/opt and $HOME/.local/bin
  exposes: stable symlinks under $HOME/.local/bin

dotfiles
  owns: tracked $HOME state
  manager: yadm first, bare Git possible
  source transform: none
```

## Current flow

```text
acquire repo
  -> strap/bootstrap
      -> detect host class
      -> seed shell env
      -> install system packages/deps
      -> install userland release tools
      -> doctor
```

## Quick start

Dry-run everything:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run
```

Run only the non-invasive checks:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run --stages detect,env,doctor
```

Install host packages:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages pkgs
```

Install userland release tools:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland
```

The `home/` tree is the source overlay for yadm-tracked dotfiles, but `strap/bootstrap` does not manage it.

## Direct-HOME migration status

The `home/` tree has been converted away from chezmoi source filenames:

```text
dot_*        -> .* 
executable_* -> real executable name + mode 0755
readonly_*   -> real filename
empty_*      -> real filename
```

That makes `home/` a real `$HOME` overlay rather than a chezmoi source tree.

## Bootstrap shell

Bootstrap scripts run non-interactively under `bash`.

`zsh` is a system dependency, not a bootstrap prerequisite. It is installed by the package-manager stage and becomes the login shell only after your shell config is in place.

## Host classes

Only two base classes are active:

| Class | Package adapter | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `arch-base` | `pacman` | Arch, Artix, EndeavourOS-style systems |

# `_404` host bootstrap + dotfiles source

This repository is now split by authority boundary:

```text
strap/   host bootstrap and activation adapter
home/    direct $HOME overlay for yadm tracking
```

Bootstrap a new host with:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fatb4f/_404/main/init.sh | bash
```

## Contract

```text
host-bootstrap
  owns: bash-only bootstrap runner, environment seed, system packages, userland tool install, doctor
  scope: arch-base | debian-base

userland installer
  owns: GitHub release artifacts under $HOME/.local/opt and $HOME/.local/bin
  exposes: stable symlinks under $HOME/.local/bin

dotfiles
  owns: tracked $HOME state
  manager: yadm first, bare Git possible
  source transform: none
```

## Current flow

```text
acquire repo
  -> strap/bootstrap
      -> detect host class
      -> seed shell env
      -> install system packages/deps
      -> install userland release tools
      -> doctor
```

## Quick start

Dry-run everything:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run
```

Run only the non-invasive checks:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run --stages detect,env,doctor
```

Install host packages:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages pkgs
```

Install userland release tools:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland
```

The `home/` tree is the source overlay for yadm-tracked dotfiles, but `strap/bootstrap` does not manage it.

## Direct-HOME migration status

The `home/` tree has been converted away from chezmoi source filenames:

```text
dot_*        -> .* 
executable_* -> real executable name + mode 0755
readonly_*   -> real filename
empty_*      -> real filename
```

That makes `home/` a real `$HOME` overlay rather than a chezmoi source tree.

## Bootstrap shell

Bootstrap scripts run non-interactively under `bash`.

`zsh` is a system dependency, not a bootstrap prerequisite. It is installed by the package-manager stage and becomes the login shell only after your shell config is in place.

## Host classes

Only two base classes are active:

| Class | Package adapter | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `arch-base` | `pacman` | Arch, Artix, EndeavourOS-style systems |
| `debian-base` | `apt-get` | Debian, Ubuntu, Penguin/Borealis-style systems |

ChromeOS/Penguin-specific integration should be added later as an overlay on `debian-base`.

## Important files

```text
strap/bootstrap                         entrypoint
strap/src/init.sh                       coordinator
strap/src/lib/which_host.sh             host-class detection
strap/src/lib/pkgs/arch-base.pkgs       Arch package overlay
strap/src/lib/pkgs/debian-base.pkgs     Debian package overlay
strap/src/lib/userland/tools.tsv        GitHub release userland tools
strap/contracts/*.cue                   authority contracts
home/                                   direct $HOME overlay
```

## Next cut

The next high-signal work is to harden the GitHub release installer:

1. add checksums/attestation fields to `strap/src/lib/pkgs/artifacts.json`
2. split `penguin` into a `debian-base + chromeos-vm` overlay
3. add a `bare-git` dotfile backend beside the current `yadm` backend

The `arch-usb` workflow now assembles a Submarine-shaped external disk image
from a cached `arch-rootfs.ext4` artifact and the `submarine-x86.kpart`
payload. The rootfs stage is built separately before image assembly, and the
final image uses an ext4 root partition.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

doctor() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local host_class=${2:?host_class}
  local failures=0
  local local_bin=${_404_LOCAL_BIN:-$HOME/.local/bin}

  printf 'host_class=%s\n' "$host_class"
  printf 'home=%s\n' "$HOME"
  printf 'xdg_config_home=%s\n' "${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}"
  printf 'xdg_data_home=%s\n' "${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}"
  printf 'local_bin=%s\n' "$local_bin"

  local cmd
  for cmd in bash git curl jq gh npm bun ya yadm zsh chsh; do
    if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      printf 'ok command %s -> %s\n' "$cmd" "$(command -v "$cmd")"
    else
      printf 'missing command %s\n' "$cmd"
      if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
        failures=$((failures + 1))
      fi
    fi
  done

  if [[ -d "$local_bin" ]]; then
    printf 'ok local_bin exists\n'
  else
    printf 'missing local_bin directory\n'
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
      failures=$((failures + 1))
    fi
  fi

  local legacy_count
  legacy_count="$(find "$project_root/home" -type f \
    \( -name 'dot_*' -o -name 'executable_*' -o -name 'readonly_*' -o -name 'empty_*' \) \
    2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
  if [[ "$legacy_count" == 0 ]]; then
    printf 'ok direct-home filenames: no chezmoi source prefixes under home/\n'
  else
    printf 'fail direct-home filenames: %s legacy chezmoi-style names remain under home/\n' "$legacy_count"
    failures=$((failures + 1))
  fi

  if [[ -r "$project_root/home/.zshenv" && -r "$project_root/home/.config/zsh/.zshenv" && -r "$project_root/home/.config/zsh/.zshrc" ]]; then
    printf 'ok zsh dotfiles exist in home overlay\n'
  else
    printf 'warn zsh dotfiles missing from home overlay\n'
  fi

  if [[ -r "$project_root/home/.config/nvim/init.lua" ]]; then
    printf 'ok nvim config exists in home overlay\n'
  else
    printf 'warn nvim config missing from home overlay\n'
  fi

  if command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf 'ok bun available for javascript lsp tooling\n'
  fi

  if command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if [[ -d "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}" ]]; then
      yadm status --short || true
    else
      printf 'yadm_repo=missing\n'
    fi
  fi

  if ((failures > 0)); then
    printf 'doctor_failures=%s\n' "$failures"
    return 1
  fi

  printf 'doctor_ok=1\n'
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

doctor() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local host_class=${2:?host_class}
  local failures=0
  local local_bin=${_404_LOCAL_BIN:-$HOME/.local/bin}

  printf 'host_class=%s\n' "$host_class"
  printf 'home=%s\n' "$HOME"
  printf 'xdg_config_home=%s\n' "${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}"
  printf 'xdg_data_home=%s\n' "${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}"
  printf 'local_bin=%s\n' "$local_bin"

  local cmd
  for cmd in bash git curl jq gh npm bun ya yadm zsh chsh; do
    if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      printf 'ok command %s -> %s\n' "$cmd" "$(command -v "$cmd")"
    else
      printf 'missing command %s\n' "$cmd"
      if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
        failures=$((failures + 1))
      fi
    fi
  done

  if [[ -d "$local_bin" ]]; then
    printf 'ok local_bin exists\n'
  else
    printf 'missing local_bin directory\n'
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
      failures=$((failures + 1))
    fi
  fi

  local legacy_count
  legacy_count="$(find "$project_root/home" -type f \
    \( -name 'dot_*' -o -name 'executable_*' -o -name 'readonly_*' -o -name 'empty_*' \) \
    2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
  if [[ "$legacy_count" == 0 ]]; then
    printf 'ok direct-home filenames: no chezmoi source prefixes under home/\n'
  else
    printf 'fail direct-home filenames: %s legacy chezmoi-style names remain under home/\n' "$legacy_count"
    failures=$((failures + 1))
  fi

  if [[ -r "$project_root/home/.zshenv" && -r "$project_root/home/.config/zsh/.zshenv" && -r "$project_root/home/.config/zsh/.zshrc" ]]; then
    printf 'ok zsh dotfiles exist in home overlay\n'
  else
    printf 'warn zsh dotfiles missing from home overlay\n'
  fi

  if [[ -r "$project_root/home/.config/nvim/init.lua" ]]; then
    printf 'ok nvim config exists in home overlay\n'
  else
    printf 'warn nvim config missing from home overlay\n'
  fi

  if command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf 'ok bun available for javascript lsp tooling\n'
  fi

  if command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if [[ -d "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}" ]]; then
      yadm status --short || true
    else
      printf 'yadm_repo=missing\n'
    fi
  fi

  if ((failures > 0)); then
    printf 'doctor_failures=%s\n' "$failures"
    return 1
  fi

  printf 'doctor_ok=1\n'
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

REPO_URL="${_404_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/fatb4f/_404.git}"
REPO_ROOT="${_404_REPO_ROOT:-${HOME}/.local/share/opt/_404}"

die() {
  printf 'error: %s\n' "$*" >&2
  exit 1
}

have() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

install_git() {
  if have git; then
    return 0
  fi

  if have apt-get; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y git
    return 0
  fi

  if have pacman; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm git
    return 0
  fi

  die 'no package manager available to install git'
}

checkout_repo() {
  local repo_parent
  repo_parent="$(dirname -- "$REPO_ROOT")"
  mkdir -p -- "$repo_parent"

  if [[ -d "$REPO_ROOT/.git" ]]; then
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" fetch --all --prune
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" pull --ff-only
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ -e "$REPO_ROOT" ]] && [[ -n "$(find "$REPO_ROOT" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)" ]]; then
    die "refusing to reuse non-git directory: $REPO_ROOT"
  fi

  rm -rf -- "$REPO_ROOT"
  git clone "$REPO_URL" "$REPO_ROOT"
}

main() {
  install_git
  checkout_repo
  exec bash "$REPO_ROOT/strap/bootstrap" "$@"
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

doctor() {
  local project_root=${1:?project_root}
  local host_class=${2:?host_class}
  local failures=0
  local local_bin=${_404_LOCAL_BIN:-$HOME/.local/bin}

  printf 'host_class=%s\n' "$host_class"
  printf 'home=%s\n' "$HOME"
  printf 'xdg_config_home=%s\n' "${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}"
  printf 'xdg_data_home=%s\n' "${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}"
  printf 'local_bin=%s\n' "$local_bin"

  local cmd
  for cmd in bash git curl jq gh npm bun ya yadm zsh chsh; do
    if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      printf 'ok command %s -> %s\n' "$cmd" "$(command -v "$cmd")"
    else
      printf 'missing command %s\n' "$cmd"
      if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
        failures=$((failures + 1))
      fi
    fi
  done

  if [[ -d "$local_bin" ]]; then
    printf 'ok local_bin exists\n'
  else
    printf 'missing local_bin directory\n'
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-0}" != 1 ]]; then
      failures=$((failures + 1))
    fi
  fi

  local legacy_count
  legacy_count="$(find "$project_root/home" -type f \
    \( -name 'dot_*' -o -name 'executable_*' -o -name 'readonly_*' -o -name 'empty_*' \) \
    2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
  if [[ "$legacy_count" == 0 ]]; then
    printf 'ok direct-home filenames: no chezmoi source prefixes under home/\n'
  else
    printf 'fail direct-home filenames: %s legacy chezmoi-style names remain under home/\n' "$legacy_count"
    failures=$((failures + 1))
  fi

  if [[ -r "$project_root/home/.zshenv" && -r "$project_root/home/.config/zsh/.zshenv" && -r "$project_root/home/.config/zsh/.zshrc" ]]; then
    printf 'ok zsh dotfiles exist in home overlay\n'
  else
    printf 'warn zsh dotfiles missing from home overlay\n'
  fi

  if [[ -r "$project_root/home/.config/nvim/init.lua" ]]; then
    printf 'ok nvim config exists in home overlay\n'
  else
    printf 'warn nvim config missing from home overlay\n'
  fi

  if command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf 'ok bun available for javascript lsp tooling\n'
  fi

  if command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if [[ -d "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}" ]]; then
      yadm status --short || true
    else
      printf 'yadm_repo=missing\n'
    fi
  fi

  if ((failures > 0)); then
    printf 'doctor_failures=%s\n' "$failures"
    return 1
  fi

  printf 'doctor_ok=1\n'
}

  fi

  if [[ -r "$project_root/home/.zshenv" && -r "$project_root/home/.config/zsh/.zshenv" && -r "$project_root/home/.config/zsh/.zshrc" ]]; then
    printf 'ok zsh dotfiles exist in home overlay\n'
  else
    printf 'warn zsh dotfiles missing from home overlay\n'
  fi

  if [[ -r "$project_root/home/.config/nvim/init.lua" ]]; then
    printf 'ok nvim config exists in home overlay\n'
  else
    printf 'warn nvim config missing from home overlay\n'
  fi

  if command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    printf 'ok bun available for javascript lsp tooling\n'
  fi

  if command -v yadm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if [[ -d "${YADM_REPO:-${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/yadm/repo.git}" ]]; then
      yadm status --short || true
    else
      printf 'yadm_repo=missing\n'
    fi
  fi

  if ((failures > 0)); then
    printf 'doctor_failures=%s\n' "$failures"
    return 1
  fi

  printf 'doctor_ok=1\n'
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

REPO_URL="${_404_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/fatb4f/_404.git}"
REPO_ROOT="${_404_REPO_ROOT:-${HOME}/.local/share/opt/_404}"

die() {
  printf 'error: %s\n' "$*" >&2
  exit 1
}

have() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

install_git() {
  if have git; then
    return 0
  fi

  if have apt-get; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get update
    sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y git
    return 0
  fi

  if have pacman; then
    have sudo || die 'sudo is required to install git'
    sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm git
    return 0
  fi

  die 'no package manager available to install git'
}

checkout_repo() {
  local repo_parent
  repo_parent="$(dirname -- "$REPO_ROOT")"
  mkdir -p -- "$repo_parent"

  if [[ -d "$REPO_ROOT/.git" ]]; then
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" fetch --all --prune
    git -C "$REPO_ROOT" pull --ff-only
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ -e "$REPO_ROOT" ]] && [[ -n "$(find "$REPO_ROOT" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)" ]]; then
    die "refusing to reuse non-git directory: $REPO_ROOT"
  fi

  rm -rf -- "$REPO_ROOT"
  git clone "$REPO_URL" "$REPO_ROOT"
}

main() {
  install_git
  checkout_repo
  exec bash "$REPO_ROOT/.local/opt/_404/init.sh" "$@"
}

main "$@"

Chunk ID: af7c75
Wall time: 0.0776 seconds

Output:
# `_404` host bootstrap + dotfiles source

This repository is now split by authority boundary:

```text
strap/   host bootstrap and activation adapter
home/    direct $HOME overlay for yadm tracking
```

Bootstrap a new host with:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fatb4f/_404/main/init.sh | bash
```

## Contract

```text
host-bootstrap
  owns: bash-only bootstrap runner, environment seed, system packages, userland tool install, doctor
  scope: arch-base | debian-base

userland installer
  owns: GitHub release artifacts under $HOME/.local/opt and $HOME/.local/bin
  exposes: stable symlinks under $HOME/.local/bin

dotfiles
  owns: tracked $HOME state
  manager: yadm first, bare Git possible
  source transform: none
```

## Current flow

```text
acquire repo
  -> strap/bootstrap
      -> detect host class
      -> seed shell env
      -> install system packages/deps
      -> install userland release tools
      -> doctor
```

## Quick start

Dry-run everything:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run
```

Run only the non-invasive checks:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run --stages detect,env,doctor
```

Install host packages:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages pkgs
```

Install userland release tools:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland
```

The `home/` tree is the source overlay for yadm-tracked dotfiles, but `strap/bootstrap` does not manage it.

## Direct-HOME migration status

The `home/` tree has been converted away from chezmoi source filenames:

```text
dot_*        -> .* 
executable_* -> real executable name + mode 0755
readonly_*   -> real filename
empty_*      -> real filename
```

That makes `home/` a real `$HOME` overlay rather than a chezmoi source tree.

## Bootstrap shell

Bootstrap scripts run non-interactively under `bash`.

`zsh` is a system dependency, not a bootstrap prerequisite. It is installed by the package-manager stage and becomes the login shell only after your shell config is in place.

## Host classes

Only two base classes are active:

| Class | Package adapter | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `arch-base` | `pacman` | Arch, Artix, EndeavourOS-style systems |
| `debian-base` | `apt-get` | Debian, Ubuntu, Penguin/Borealis-style systems |

ChromeOS/Penguin-specific integration should be added later as an overlay on `debian-base`.

## Important files

```text
strap/bootstrap                         entrypoint
strap/src/init.sh                       coordinator
strap/src/lib/which_host.sh             host-class detection
strap/src/lib/pkgs/arch-base.pkgs       Arch package overlay
strap/src/lib/pkgs/debian-base.pkgs     Debian package overlay
strap/src/lib/userland/tools.tsv        GitHub release userland tools
strap/contracts/*.cue                   authority contracts
home/                                   direct $HOME overlay
```

## Next cut

The next high-signal work is to harden the GitHub release installer:

1. add checksums/attestation fields to `strap/src/lib/pkgs/artifacts.json`
2. split `penguin` into a `debian-base + chromeos-vm` overlay
3. add a `bare-git` dotfile backend beside the current `yadm` backend

The `arch-usb` workflow now assembles a Submarine-shaped external disk image
from a cached `arch-rootfs.ext4` artifact and the `submarine-x86.kpart`
payload. The rootfs stage is built separately before image assembly, and the
final image uses an ext4 root partition.

# `_404` strap

host-bootstrap

The dotfile authority should track `$HOME` directly with `yadm` or a bare Git repo.

## Host classes

Only two base classes are supported for now:

| Host class | Adapter |
|---|---|
| `arch-base` | `sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm` |
| `debian-base` | `sudo -n apt-get`, `DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive` |

Penguin/Borealis-style Debian environments collapse to `debian-base`; ChromeOS-specific integration should be added later as an overlay, not as a third base class.

## Non-interactive contract

`strap/bootstrap` runs under Bash and never prompts intentionally.

Package managers use non-interactive flags:

```text
arch-base   -> sudo -n pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm
debian-base -> sudo -n env DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y
```

Before install, each base-package entry is smoke-tested against the package manager to make sure it resolves in the current distro metadata. If a package does not resolve, bootstrap fails closed before the install step.

## Commands

Dry-run the full flow:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --dry-run
```

Run only host detection:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages detect
```

Seed Bash-compatible env files and run doctor:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages env,doctor
```

Install system packages only:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages pkgs
```

Install userland release tools only:

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland
```

## Package-manager deps

`jq`, `gh`, and `npm` are package-manager dependencies, not userland release artifacts.
There is no shared `common.pkgs` layer; package names are adapter-specific.
`base-devel` is the shared build-tool intent; on Arch it resolves to `base-devel`, and on Debian it resolves through `build-essential`.

Concrete package lists:

```text
strap/src/lib/pkgs/arch-base.pkgs
strap/src/lib/pkgs/debian-base.pkgs
```

The package lists stay host-specific because Arch and Debian package names diverge.

## Userland artifacts

Userland packages are declared in:

```text
strap/src/lib/pkgs/artifacts.json
```

The installer filters that manifest with `jq`:

```bash
jq -c '.artifacts[] | select((.enabled // true) == true)' \
  strap/src/lib/pkgs/artifacts.json
```

For each selected row, it downloads the latest matching GitHub release asset with:

```bash
gh release download -R "$repo" --pattern "$pkg" --dir "$tmp"
```

Before downloading, the installer smoke-tests the pattern against the latest
release asset list from the manager and fails closed if nothing matches.

Default locations:

```text
~/.local/bin                         executable files and symlinks
~/.local/opt                         extracted application trees for opt installs
$XDG_CACHE_HOME/_404/artifacts       release download cache
$XDG_STATE_HOME/_404/userland-installed.tsv
```

The receipt TSV records: `name`, `repo`, `pkg`, `artifact`, `strategy`, `bins`, `status`.

dotfiles

  source transform: none
```

```text
acquire repo
  -> strap/bootstrap
      -> detect host class
      -> seed shell env

      -> install userland release tools
      -> doctor

strap/bootstrap --dry-run

```bash
strap/bootstrap --stages userland

The `home/` tree is the source overlay for yadm-tracked dotfiles, but `strap/bootstrap` does not manage it.

## Direct-HOME migration status

The `home/` tree has been converted away from chezmoi source filenames:

```text
dot_*        -> .* 
executable_* -> real executable name + mode 0755
readonly_*   -> real filename
empty_*      -> real filename
```

That makes `home/` a real `$HOME` overlay rather than a chezmoi source tree.

## Bootstrap shell

Bootstrap scripts run non-interactively under `bash`.

`zsh` is a system dependency, not a bootstrap prerequisite. It is installed by the package-manager stage and becomes the login shell only after your shell config is in place.

## Host classes

Only two base classes are active:

| Class | Package adapter | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `arch-base` | `pacman` | Arch, Artix, EndeavourOS-style systems |
| `debian-base` | `apt-get` | Debian, Ubuntu, Penguin/Borealis-style systems |

ChromeOS/Penguin-specific integration should be added later as an overlay on `debian-base`.

## Important files

```text
strap/bootstrap                         entrypoint
strap/src/init.sh                       coordinator
strap/src/lib/which_host.sh             host-class detection
strap/src/lib/pkgs/arch-base.pkgs       Arch package overlay
strap/src/lib/pkgs/debian-base.pkgs     Debian package overlay
strap/src/lib/userland/tools.tsv        GitHub release userland tools
strap/contracts/*.cue                   authority contracts
home/                                   direct $HOME overlay
```

The next high-signal work is to harden the GitHub release installer:

The `arch-usb` workflow now assembles a Submarine-shaped external disk image
from a cached `arch-rootfs.ext4` artifact and the `submarine-x86.kpart`
payload. The rootfs stage is built separately before image assembly, and the
final image uses an ext4 root partition.
